titlestmt.titleproper: Chicago, Ill. Irving Park Immanuel Congregational Church records, 1906-1984.
titlestmt.author: Zachary Bodnar

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday May 8, 2026 at 19:21

did.unittitle: Chicago, Ill. Irving Park Immanuel Congregational Church records, 1906-1984.
did.abstract: The Irving Park Immanuel Congregational Church was established in 1905 and Rev. Ira Milton Grey served as the church's first minister. The church building was constructed between 1907 and 1925. A parsonage was purchased in 1950. The church joined the United Church of Christ. The church dissolved in 1998. This collection contains membership records and administrative records and includes meeting minutes, vital membership records, and correspondence.

corpname.part: Irving Park Immanuel Congregational Church (Chicago, Ill.)

accessrestrict.head: Restrictions on Access
accessrestrict.p: Access to this collection is unrestricted and open to the public.

userestrict.head: Restrictions on Use
userestrict.p: Items in this collection are subject to U.S. Copyright Law. It is the responsibility of the researcher to determine the copyright status of collection items and to secure any permissions necessary for their reproduction and publication. Requests for permission to publish material must be discussed with the archivist or librarian.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: [Identification of item], in the Chicago, Ill. Irving Park Immanuel Congregational Church records, 1906-1984, RG5593. The Congregational Library & Archives, Boston, MA.

acqinfo.head: Acquisition Information
acqinfo.p: Materials were donated to the Congregational Library & Archives by the Illinois Conference of the United Church of Christ in December 2024; accession number 2024-46.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: This collection was processed by Zachary Bodnar, April 2026, according to the rules of Describing Archives: A Content Standard.

bioghist.head: Historical Note
bioghist.p: The history of the Irving Park Immanuel Congregational Church in Chicago's East Irving Park district began with the establishment of a Sunday school in 1896. The Sunday school was established by Rev. Jesse Brooks, then the pastor of the Irving Park Reformed Church. From the Sunday school community arose a desire to establish a Congregational Church and in 1905 the Irving Park Immanuel Congregational Church was established. 35 founding members signed the church's charter in 1906. Rev. Ira Milton Grey served as the church's first minister.

The church building was constructed at the corner of West Berteau Avenue and North Troy Street. At first the congregation inhabited a temporary structure on the lot from 1907 until 1910. In 1910, the basement of the permanent building was completed and the congregation used that space for worship services until the building's construction was completed in 1925. In 1928, an Austin organ was installed in the church. The building underwent renovations between 1947 and 1949 and a parsonage was purchased at 4122 North Richmond Street in 1950.
The church joined the United Church of Christ after the denomination's formation in 1957. The church dissolved in 1998. The church building is still used as a church today.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This collection contains membership records and some administrative records related to the Irving Park Immanuel Congregational Church in Chicago, Illinois. The collection includes meeting minutes, membership registers, baptismal records, marriage records, death records, and correspondence.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials in this collection have been arranged in chronological order by start date.

corpname.part: Irving Park Immanuel Congregational Church (Chicago, Ill.)

separatedmaterial.head: Separated Materials
separatedmaterial.p: Loose materials found at the start of this volume were sepparated and moved into a folder titled "Miscellaneous membership records, 1964, 1980-1984" for preservation purposes.

separatedmaterial.head: Separated Materials
separatedmaterial.p: Materials in this folder were removed from the front of the volume titled "Church records, 1961-1984" for preservation purposes.
